question: Explain the HEART framework and its five dimensions.
expectedPoints: Happiness - user satisfaction and attitude
Engagement - level of user interaction and involvement
Adoption - rate of new user acquisition
Retention - frequency of user return/repeat usage
Task success - effectiveness in completing user goals

question: What are SMART goals in UI/UX design? Why are they important?
expectedPoints: SMART = Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound
Provide clear direction and focus for design projects
Enable tracking of design success objectively
Help align design with business objectives
Set realistic expectations for stakeholders

question: Differentiate between quantitative and qualitative metrics with examples.
expectedPoints: Quantitative = numerical data (conversion rate, time on task)
Qualitative = descriptive data (user feedback, interview insights)
Quantitative examples: bounce rate, click-through rate, NPS score
Qualitative examples: user quotes, satisfaction comments
Both needed: numbers explain WHAT, feedback explains WHY

question: What is A/B testing? Describe its significance in UI/UX design.
expectedPoints: A/B testing compares two versions of a design element
Version A is control (original), Version B is variant
Users randomly assigned to each version
Statistical significance determines winner
Reduces risk of negative changes, validates design decisions

question: Explain the System Usability Scale (SUS) and interpret SUS scores.
expectedPoints: SUS is a 10-question standardized usability survey
Scores range from 0-100
Score above 68 is above average
Score above 80.3 indicates excellent usability
Score below 50 indicates serious usability issues

question: List and explain five key UX metrics used in measuring product success.
expectedPoints: Task Completion Rate - success in user goals
Time on Task - efficiency of完成任务
Error Rate - usability problems
Engagement Rate - user interaction level
Retention/Churn Rate - user loyalty

question: What is Net Promoter Score (NPS)? How is it calculated and interpreted?
expectedPoints: NPS measures user loyalty and satisfaction
Users rate likelihood to recommend (0-10)
Promoters (9-10), Passives (7-8), Detractors (0-6)
NPS = % Promoters - % Detractors (range: -100 to +100)
Above 50 is excellent, above 70 is world-class

question: Describe the process of setting effective UX targets for a mobile application. Include relevant frameworks and considerations.
expectedPoints: Start with business objectives and user research
Apply HEART framework to identify metric categories
Set SMART goals for each metric
Establish baseline metrics through research
Set realistic targets based on industry benchmarks
Prioritize metrics using impact vs effort matrix
Create measurement plan and tracking cadence
Review and adjust targets periodically

question: Explain the relationship between UX metrics and business metrics. How can design decisions be justified using data?
expectedPoints: UX metrics directly impact business KPIs
Good UX increases conversion and revenue
Reduced support costs through better usability
Customer retention drives lifetime value
Data-driven design wins stakeholder trust
A/B testing provides concrete evidence
ROI calculations link design to money saved/earned
Case studies with before/after metrics

question: Design a comprehensive measurement plan for an e-commerce checkout flow. Specify the metrics, targets, and methods.
expectedPoints: Key metrics: Cart abandonment rate, Checkout completion rate, Time on checkout, Error rate, Customer satisfaction
Baseline measurement through analytics
Target setting: Reduce abandonment by 15%, Increase completion to 70%
Methods: Funnel analysis, Heatmaps, User testing, Surveys
Tools: Google Analytics, Hotjar, Optimizely
A/B test checkout variations
Monitor post-launch and iterate
